tracks.title: The Stardust Engine (Live in Chicago)
tracks.loreNote: The historical 'Smoking Gun' proving they were siblings. The band despises this recording because Apex maliciously buried Cassidy's synths in the mix.

tracks.title: Neon Hearts (Live in Chicago)
tracks.loreNote: A mandatory setlist inclusion to promote the tour. Ryan plays the 'good soldier,' delivering a polished synth-pop performance despite chafing at the safe direction.

tracks.title: Dancing in the Neon (Live in Chicago)
tracks.loreNote: Played at a frantic, rushed tempo to fit their strict 30-minute opening slot. A studio-mandated inclusion to win over the headliner's pop audience.

tracks.title: If You Walked Away (Live in Chicago)
tracks.loreNote: A terrifying performance for Cassidy, who experienced the lyrics as a genuine fear of abandonment. Ryan held her for 20 minutes backstage afterward to ground her.

tracks.title: Light Up the Night (Live in Chicago)
tracks.loreNote: Apex's failed 1987 'Big Bet.' The band was under immense pressure to execute this perfectly every night, proving they could play the corporate game even if they hated it.

tracks.title: My Anchor Holds (Live in Chicago)
tracks.loreNote: A sibling survival anthem disguised as a romantic ballad. The 'Ironheads' in the crowd knew this was a public promise to protect each other.

tracks.title: Electric Color (Live in Chicago)
tracks.loreNote: The frantic, joyful grand finale. With no time for an encore, they used this massive hit to try and steal the show from the headliner.
